en.extract:
The azure-breasted pitta (Pitta steerii) is a species of bird in the family Pittidae. It is a striking and colorful bird having colors of red, azure, green, black and white, It is endemic to the islands of Mindanao, Bohol, Leyte and Samar in the Philippines. Along with the Whiskered pitta, it is one of two endemic pittas in the country.  Its natural habitat is tropical moist lowland forest.  It is threatened by habitat loss.


Description and taxonomy


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognized:

P. s. steeri – Found on Mindanao; Darker and has a stronger green tinge
P. s. coelestis – Found on Bohol, Leyte and Samar; Paler in colour


Ecology and behavior
Feeds on insects and worms. Forages on the ground, fallen logs and boulders, flicking over dead leaves with its bill. 
Breeding  basically unknown. On Bohol, most vocal active in April to June, which serves as the breeding season for a large majority of Philippine birds. Juveniles seen on Samar and Bohol in the months of Jun and July. Nest is still undescribed.


Habitat and conservation status
It is found in tropical moist lowland forest with dense understory up to 1,000 meters above sea level but more common below 600 meters. It prefers forests with limestone outcrops.
IUCN has assessed this bird as Vulnerable with the population being estimated at 2,500 to 9,999 mature individuals remaining. This species' main threat is habitat loss with wholesale clearance of forest habitats as a result of logging, agricultural conversion and mining activities occurring within the range. The most affected part of its range is Bohol which only has 4% forest cover remaining.
Occurs in a few protected areas in Rajah Sikatuna Protected Landscape in Bohol and Samar Island Natural Park but actual protection and enforcement from illegal logging and hunting are lax.
Conservation actions proposed are to do fieldwork and exploration to better understand its status and distribution. It's also recommended that its preference for limestone areas be further studied.


Gallery


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet

The '''azure-breasted pitta''' ('''''Pitta steerii''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pittidae]]. It is a striking and colorful bird having colors of red, azure, green, black and white, It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the islands of [[Mindanao]], [[Bohol]], [[Leyte]] and [[Samar]] in the [[Philippines]]. Along with the [[Whiskered pitta]], it is one of two endemic pittas in the country.  Its natural [[habitat]] is [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest|tropical moist lowland forest]].  It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

== Description and taxonomy ==
[[File:BirdsAsiaJohnGoVGoul 0304, b.jpg|left|thumb|An illustration by [[John Gould]]]]

=== Subspecies ===
Two [[subspecies]] are recognized:

* ''P. s. steeri'' – Found on [[Mindanao]]; Darker and has a stronger green tinge
* ''P. s. coelestis'' – Found on [[Bohol]], [[Leyte]] and [[Samar]]; Paler in colour<ref name=":0" />

== Ecology and behavior ==
Feeds on insects and worms. Forages on the ground, fallen logs and boulders, flicking over dead leaves with its bill. 

Breeding  basically unknown. On Bohol, most vocal active in April to June, which serves as the breeding season for a large majority of Philippine birds. Juveniles seen on Samar and Bohol in the months of Jun and July. Nest is still undescribed.<ref name="BotW">{{Cite journal |last=Erritzoe |first=Johannes |date=2020 |title=Azure-breasted Pitta (Pitta steerii), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/azbpit1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.azbpit1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Habitat and conservation status ==
It is found in tropical moist lowland forest with dense understory up to 1,000 meters above sea level but more common below 600 meters. It prefers forests with [[limestone]] outcrops.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|last=Allen|first=Desmond|title=Birds of the Philippines|publisher=Lynx and Birdlife Guides International|year=2020|location=Barcelona|pages=234–235}}</ref>

IUCN has assessed this bird as [[Vulnerable species|Vulnerable]] with the population being estimated at 2,500 to 9,999 mature individuals remaining. This species' main threat is [[habitat loss]] with wholesale clearance of forest habitats as a result of logging, agricultural conversion and mining activities occurring within the range. The most affected part of its range is [[Bohol]] which only has 4% forest cover remaining.

Occurs in a few protected areas in [[Rajah Sikatuna Protected Landscape]] in Bohol and [[Samar Island Natural Park]] but actual protection and enforcement from [[illegal logging]] and hunting are lax.

Conservation actions proposed are to do fieldwork and exploration to better understand its status and distribution. It's also recommended that its preference for limestone areas be further studied.<ref name="BotW"/>

zh.extract:
蓝胸八色鸫（学名：Pitta steerii），是八色鸫科八色鸫属的一种，为菲律宾的特有种。全球活动范围约为118,000平方千米。该物种的保护状况被评为易危。
蓝胸八色鸫的平均体重约为92.0克。栖息地为亚热带或热带的湿润低地林。


参考文献
